title: Delicate Tunisian Snapper Fish Tagine
description: A mustardy and pillowy Tunisian dish featuring snapper fish, perfect for a date night, cooked to perfection in a bamboo steamer using a bain-marie method.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine onion, garlic, mustard, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
Place the snapper fish fillets in a bamboo steamer lined with parchment paper.
Brush the blended onion mixture evenly over both sides of the fish fillets.
Add the dates, chicken broth, and lemon juice to the blender and blend until the dates are well chopped.
Pour the date mixture over the fish fillets in the steamer.
Place the steamer over a pot of boiling water, making sure the water level is below the bottom of the steamer.
Cover the pot with a lid and steam the fish for 12 minutes.
Remove the steamer from the pot and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the fish hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley and lemon wedges.
